topics.title: Europe faces another heatwave test
topics.summary: Italy and the Balkans are under red heat alerts as Europe’s severe June heatwave raises wildfire concerns and strains daily life, travel and health systems.
topics.discussionPrompt: When heat starts changing work, travel and school plans, do you think cities should adapt quickly, or do people still treat it like a temporary nuisance?

topics.title: Canada reaches World Cup last 16
topics.summary: Canada advanced in the World Cup after Stephen Eustáquio scored a stoppage-time winner against South Africa, sending the co-hosts into the last 16.
topics.discussionPrompt: A late goal can turn a whole country into soccer fans for a day. Do you like those dramatic finishes, or are they too stressful?

topics.title: Toronto stocks edge higher
topics.summary: Canada’s main stock index opened slightly higher as Middle East worries eased, though investors stayed cautious ahead of the USMCA review and weakness in precious metals.
topics.discussionPrompt: Markets can react to faraway events almost instantly. Do you follow that kind of news, or only notice when it affects prices close to home?

topics.title: Wimbledon keeps football off-screen
topics.summary: Wimbledon says World Cup matches will not be shown on the grounds, even in the players’ lounge, as tennis and soccer compete for attention.
topics.discussionPrompt: Do you like big events keeping their own atmosphere, or should places like Wimbledon embrace whatever else everyone wants to watch?
